definition:
Hopper hashing occurs when a device depends on a specific ticking order of hoppers for operation. This is a problem because hoppers are block entities, which are ticked in a location dependent but unpredictable order. A device that relies on this order can fail when placed in different locations. 

## More Details
This occurs because the block entity list [`LevelTicks.allContainers`](https://mcsrc.dev/#1/1.21.11_unobfuscated/net/minecraft/world/ticks/LevelTicks#L34) is a [Long2ObjectOpenHashMap](https://javadoc.io/doc/it.unimi.dsi/fastutil/latest/it/unimi/dsi/fastutil/longs/Long2ObjectOpenHashMap.html) which doesn't guarantee order.
